entry.title: eSPP complex BOD with exclusive local suppliers
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I am fairly new to the eSPP solution and need some guidance with a topic.

We are implementing a stand-alone eSPP solution with connections to various ECC systems.

We are busy implementing a solution which as a fair sized and complexity BOD (and multiples).

The one challenge we are facing, is that we have some products that can be supplied to a location from a exclusive/unique supplier in our BOD, normally on the lowest level. The product number is the same in the whole network, and the parts supplied from the exclusive/unique supplier can not be redistributed in the network, It can only be sold on that level of the BOD (atleast until further notice).

Any ideas on how to setup this scenario, within the standard configuration?

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Add Attachment to ouptut in Sales Quotation
entry.content: In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, attachments in “Manage Sales Quotation” don’t appear in the output. To include them, send manually, use Smart/Adobe Forms, or apply a custom enhancement/API.

entry.title: Re: Default Cost Center for Postings for Statistical Project
entry.content:
What you are seeing is actually standard behavior in S/4HANA Public Cloud projects:

When you release a project with a default cost center for postings, that default is inherited by the WBS elements at the time of release.

Changing the default cost center later in the project definition does not retroactively update the cost center assignment of existing WBS elements. Each WBS element keeps the cost center it was originally linked to.

That is why your sub‑items still point to the old cost center and trigger the “no budget” error — they are still technically assigned to the previous cost center, even though you changed the project’s default.

What you can do

Check WBS Master Data
Go into each WBS element and verify the “Responsible Cost Center” field. Update it to the new cost center if required.

Mass Change Option
In Public Cloud, you can use the “Project Control” or “Project Builder” apps to perform mass changes on WBS elements. This is the recommended way if you have many WBS elements.

Budget Assignment
Ensure the new cost center has budget assigned. After updating the WBS elements, the purchase order will consume against the correct cost center budget.

entry.title: Re: Limit item with Service Entry Sheet using Self Servic...
entry.content: Update: It works by choosing 'Service' instead 'Material' in the Fiori app. This triggers the Product type group for Service and enables the GR (SES).

entry.title: Re: How to reprocess the full language installation ?
entry.content: In ECC 6.0 EHP8, to fix incomplete language installation (SMLT), re-run the SMLT transaction for the desired language. Ensure transport requests are released, then run “SMLT → Reinstall Language” to update all texts and translations. Some standard transactions may still need manual adjustments if previous install was partial.

entry.title: Re: advanced variant configuration ETO public cloud
entry.content: This was caused because the CTO profile was not set to explode the BoM, once this had been changed all was OK. Lesson learned not to be forgotten: if want ETO profile then any other profile needs to have BoM explosion set to multilevel

entry.title: SAC Planning and Datasphere: How to use Audit Logs for Analysis and Recovery
entry.content:
Introduction

Tracing published data changes in SAC is not an easy task. The default "Data Changes" application in SAC (Security -> Data Changes) is a list report that shows the full granularity of the data. Functionalities like in the data analyzer to drag and drop columns to analyze the data changes are missing. Furthermore, the data changes cannot be used to roll back the published plan data to a specific point in time. In an earlier blog post I compared the SAC model with the write optimized ADSO in SAP BW, not having the request information and inherited delta mechanism.

In this blog post I will share a solution for better analysis and recovery via change logs taking advantage of the newest seamless planning features. This solution does not necessarily require seamless planning, but I will go into detail later.

Architecture

Remarks regarding DPA / Remote Table:

  • DPA with CDI Adapter is required for real time aquisition
  • Audit Logs for a specific model can be aquired in Datasphere via CDI connection type
  • Performance of remote table is crucial - consider aquiring only the latest changes via remote table and persist older changes via data flow in Datasphere. These considerations heavily depend of your requirements, model and data volume.
  • Enforcing a dynamic filter push down via remote table on the Audit_Time field was only possible by adding bind_as_value function in the where clause: Query Parameterization: BIND_AS_PARAMETER and BIND_AS_VALUE | SAP Help Portal -> when working with remote tables always check out the actual statement that's being pushed down
  • If you don't have seamless planning yet, the audit data could be loaded via import job to SAC instead of using a live version - this would require the execution of the import job to have a look at the latest data changes
  • Latency of the view on the remote table is about 3 seconds also for empty audit tables - i guess, that's the communication overhead. Good news is that a view with filter push down on huge audit tables has a similar performance

Analyzing the data in SAC

First, I will show you a simple story for planning CostCenter - GLAccount combinations. As you can see, CostCenter A and C have a total of 200 each:

When I look at the audit log story, I can see that the sum of the delta values equals the result in the plan story:

Every dimension is available as filter criteria. For consistency reasons the "SourceVersion" (i.e. the actual version in the plan model) is a single value filter. The "Version" of the audit model is the version "live". That is, because the live connection from Datasphere to SAC is established via an own version. Furthermore, in contrast to the standard application for data changes, we have now the ability to use the new "Quick Builder" in the story to drag and drop dimensions to further analyze the data changes. We could also apply hierarchies to answer business related questions like: "Why and when did my EBIT change?"

Undo data changes / recover plan data in SAC

A critical incident would be the unintended deletion of the whole version data. In this small example I deleted the "public.Budget" data for the whole model. In the audit story the deletions are visible:

Since the audit data is in a SAC model now, the delta records can be used for a cross model copy step to undo this data change. One way to achieve it is by appending the inverted delta value in the cross model copy step. In this example model I build a cross model copy step with all possible filter criteria as parameters (also, for instance, the audit time). Those input parameters are bound to the story filters in this specific story. So, by filtering to the specific audit date in this example, the data action ("Undo Selection") - inheriting the cross model copy step - is prefilled:

After execution of the data action and reset of the story filter, the inverted delta bookings can be seen in the audit log story:

Also in the plan story, the data is visible again!

Remarks regarding cross model copy step:

  • The relevant dimensions (here: CostCenter, GLAccount) should be public in both models
  • The date dimension, by its private nature, should be generated identically in the audit model as in the plan model
  • Theoretically, fact and corresponding master data could be deleted in the plan model as part of a housekeeping task. Keep in mind that the audit logs should be deleted for those combinations aswell to avoid warning messages.
  • If you check out the data action prompt, you can see that the "SourceVersion" and the "TargetVersion" (i.e. the version in the plan model) are two different prompts. It probably never makes sence to recover data to another "TargetVersion" as the "SourceVersion". To eliminate potential risks here, the "TargetVersion" prompt could be filled via script.

Towards a Reliable Change‑Recovery Process in SAC

SAC’s open, self‑service-oriented planning allows users to adjust productive data directly, which speeds up planning but also introduces a risk to data quality, especially when self‑service stories are used in production. In our project, this led to situations where entire versions were deleted unintentionally or values were spread across all dates due to a single user action. Recovery required manual corrections based on audit logs or deletion of unintended data.

Live Versions improve the situation by capturing delta values and deletions more consistently and reducing gaps that previously occurred through import jobs. This strengthens the audit trail and provides a more reliable basis for analyzing changes and restoring data. However, the audit log is not guaranteed to remain perfectly synchronized with the planning model (entries may be missing or deleted), so it should always be viewed as a time‑limited recovery source.

The approach described in this blog uses audit data in Datasphere and SAC to make changes analyzable across dimensions and to restore data selectively using inverted deltas. This creates a practical, repeatable recovery mechanism that reduces the risks of self‑service planning in production while preserving SAC’s flexibility. It covers not only new and modified records but also deleted ones.

And for anyone at SAP working on seamless planning: the recent developments are very encouraging! Making audit logs natively accessible in Datasphere would further strengthen performance and simplify recovery scenarios.

entry.title: Naming/Set up company code ID
entry.content:
Dear Community,

since last year SAP changed the logic regarding company code ID from numerical to alphernumeric.

Meaning Company Code set up from 1010 to 10DE for e.g.

Based on our experience this logic is not aligned within the whole SAP landscape, meaning within the Starter System for e.g. alphernumeric (10DE) is mandatory but within the Customer System it was still possible to go ahead with the old logic (1010)

Has this already been aligned or is it still the case, that both ways for company code set up are possible?

The only reference we found at SAP about this topic is from 2024:

3378323 - Naming Convention of Company Codes - SAP for Me

Thx & BR

Marcel

entry.title: CK2027 Error When Marking Standard Cost Estimates in F1865 - Organizational Measure Not Allowed for
entry.content:
## Issue Description

**Error Message:** CK2027
**Error Text:**
> "In Legal Valuation the following occurred: You are trying to mark materials for period 002/2026 and company code 1510 in ledger 0L, although marking has not been allowed for this."

**System:** SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
**App Used:** F1865 - Manage Costing Runs - Estimated Costs
**Step Failed:** Marking step in costing run execution
**Business Context:** Standard cost estimate marking for period 002/2026

---

## Root Cause

The organizational measure (organizational standard) has not been enabled for **marking standard cost estimates** for the following combination:
- Company Code: 1510
- Ledger: 0L (Leading Ledger)
- Period: 002/2026
- Valuation Type: Legal Valuation

---

## Solution

### Step 1: Navigate to Organizational Standards App

**App ID to search:** Look for organizational standards/measures app (likely something like F2XXX or similar)

**Search terms in SAP Fiori Launchpad:**
- "Organizational Standards"
- "Organizational Measure"
- "Manage Organizational Standards"
- "組織標準"

### Step 2: Access the Configuration

Once in the app:

1. Filter/navigate to:
- Company Code: **1510**
- Ledger: **0L**
- Fiscal Year/Period: **002/2026**

2. Look for settings related to **Product Costing** or **Material Price Determination**

### Step 3: Enable Marking Permission

1. Click on **Company Code** or **Valuation View** in the list
2. You'll either see:
- Permitted costing variants (if already enabled), OR
- A request/button to enable organizational measure
3. Enable marking for your **Costing Variant** used in the costing run
4. Ensure **"Allow Marking"** is checked for period 002/2026
5. Save

### Step 4: Retry in F1865

1. Go back to **F1865 - Manage Costing Runs - Estimated Costs**
2. Select your costing run
3. Re-execute the **Marking** step

Error should be resolved.

---

## Additional Info Needed

**Question:** What is the exact Fiori App ID for managing organizational standards in your system? (So I can give you the exact app to use)

entry.title: Re: How in SAP S/4 HANA public cloud legal requirement fo...
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, threshold‑based tax rates (like the GST examples you listed for apparel, footwear, and luxury items) are managed through the Advanced Compliance Reporting and Tax Service framework rather than hard‑coding rates into the material master. Here’s how it works conceptually:

How threshold‑based GST rates are handled

Tax Service / Determination
Public Cloud uses the SAP Localization Hub – Tax Service (integrated with S/4HANA Cloud). Tax determination is based on condition records (similar to SD pricing) but extended with tax classification and thresholds.

Condition Technique
You define tax condition types (e.g., GST5, GST12, GST18, GST40). Access sequences can include material tax classification, product category, and transaction value. Thresholds (like Rs. 500, Rs. 1,000, Rs. 2,500) are modeled as scale values in the condition record.

Scale‑Based Tax Rates
For apparel: condition record with scale values →

  • Up to Rs. 2,500 → 5%
  • Above Rs. 2,500 → 12% (or 18% in 2025 framework).

For footwear: condition record with scale values →

  • Up to Rs. 500 → 5%
  • Above Rs. 500 → 18%.

Product Allocation / Classification
Materials are assigned to tax categories (e.g., “Apparel,” “Footwear,” “Luxury”). The system reads the transaction value (net price per piece) and applies the correct tax rate based on the scale.

Future Framework (GST 2.0, 2025/26)
New slabs (like 40% for luxury vehicles) are introduced as new condition records with thresholds. You don’t need new material masters — the same product can have multiple tax rates depending on value.

entry.title: Implementing B2B Advisory in SAP Integration Suite for Custom XML EDIFACT Scenario
entry.content:
In many organizations, B2B transactions are not always handled using pure standard formats. Over time, custom XML structures are introduced on top of EDIFACT messages to align with internal processing or EDI broker requirements.

I encountered a similar scenario in one of my recent projects. Through our EDI broker, we were consuming EDIFACT messages in an XML representation. With the upcoming S/4HANA migration, the same integrations needed to be moved to SAP Integration Suite.

Interestingly, the migration itself was not the biggest challenge. The real concern was monitoring and trading partner mantinance.

In our existing SAP PI/PO landscape, we have more than 400 active interfaces processing approximately 200,000+ transactions. Monitoring in such a landscape is already complex. Moving to Integration Suite without a structured monitoring approach would only increase the operational overhead.

Why We Considered B2B Advisory

To address this, we evaluated B2B Advisory capabilities in Integration Suite. The key expectations were:

  • Automatic payload logging retention for a defined number of days
  • Clear identification of trading partner information
  • Agreement-based processing for structured partner management
  • A dedicated monitoring environment for B2B transactions

Another important aspect was our S/4HANA clean core strategy. By leveraging B2B Advisory features properly, we see opportunities to reduce custom logic and keep the core system less cluttered with monitoring-specific enhancements.

Migration Context: From PO Without TPM to Integration Suite
In our SAP PO system, we were not using Trading Partner Management (TPM). As a result, many partner-specific handling rules were built into custom logic. While setting up B2B Advisory in Integration Suite, we started seeing clear structural benefits — especially around agreement handling and partner-based message identification. Even at this in-progress stage, the difference in approach compared to PO is quite visible.

This blog focuses on how I configured B2B Advisory to support our XML-based custom EDIFACT messages. Since we could not directly use the standard packages as-is, I made specific adjustments to adapt them to our scenario. I will walk through:

  • How the standard B2B Advisory packages behave
  • What limitations we observed with custom XML EDIFACT
  • What modifications were required
  • Key learnings during configuration

This is still work in progress, but the insights gained so far are already valuable, especially for teams moving from PI/PO to Integration Suite without prior TPM usage.

  • I have devided thie blog into 3 parts where current blog refers the configuration what we need to do in TPM (Trading partner Management such as Compnay profile, Partner profile, Identifiers, Communication configuration etc.
  • 2nd blog will be based on --> agreements and couple of features i have used.
  • 3rd blog will be how Iflows are configured and customized as per the need.

Quick Concept: How B2B Advisory & Integration works

 Above is the a explanitary flow how messgae processing works with B2B Advisory. Here the brain is TPM Setup and Iflows are executing engine. Let's break down the setup I have created within TPM.

 

TPM Setup for B2B Advisory

As explained in the processing flow, the standard B2B Advisory packages rely heavily on Trading Partner Management (TPM). The iFlow itself does not contain hardcoded partner logic. Instead, it dynamically resolves sender, receiver, and agreement details from TPM configuration at runtime. The TPM setup was structured as follows.

Company

The Company object represents our organization within the B2B landscape. In our case:

  • It reflects our enterprise entity interacting with external partners
  • This acts as the anchor for all agreements.
  • All inbound and outbound B2B agreements are defined relative to this Company.

Important consideration:
We ensured that the company identifiers align with the values extracted from the XML payload. If the identifiers do not match exactly, agreement resolution will fail.

Here I have created 2 identifiers from SAP where 1st "BTPISEDI" is the one i'm using for all outbound from SAP (Idoc always) as this is the port we have defined for IDoc in WE21. and "DS4CLNT120" for all inbound to SAP as that is the logical system for all inbound. How those will be used will be describe in coming setps.

On the continuation on Company profile, we have Identifer where we can add individual identifer and Group Identifier. Group will be used when we have the varite of identifers. 

IdentifierActual value from the payload which will be use to identify the flow
AliasRepresentation of the identifer which will be display at config level
Type SystemType System means the Source Document type in simple language
Scheme Name/CodeKind of the identifer number for internal use eg : Customer Number or Partner Profile
Agency Name/CodeKind of the identidier for the trading partner eg : DUNS number or Sender ID

Within the Trading Partner Management setup, the Systems tab allows us to define communication system details such as IDoc or Process Direct configuration parameters.

However, it is important to understand that these are not actual adapters.

The configurations maintained here do not represent runtime adapters directly. Instead, they act as logical placeholders that the standard B2B package references during execution.

How It Works Conceptually:

In TPM, we define a System for:

  • External partner side
  • Our backend system (e.g., S/4HANA)
  • Type System (System version)

Under the System definition, we maintain adapter-related attributes such as:

  • Endpoint url of the system (internet/On-Prim)
  • Location ID (if applicable)
  • Process Direct url
  • Credentails if application 

These settings are then picked up by the standard B2B iFlow at runtime. The iFlow reads the resolved Agreement --> identifies the associated Systems --> and dynamically maps the configured values into the actual adapter that is connected in the integration flow.

Learning from various hit/try : If under system purpose is maintain as test, then in case of inbound to SAP, Idoc will never post for automatic posting it should be marked at production.

Partner Profile

Next step would be maintain the system and trading partner information. Here 2 section are devided into 2 maintinance. 1st is the Communication Partner (EDI Broker/Trading Partner Connected System/any other middleware) and Trading Partner (your actual customer/vendor).

Quick concept on Communication Partner :

  • protocol supported Process Direct and AS2.
  • only connection details are requried under communication partner.
  • technical placeholder for connection.
  • certificates/signatature (for signing and encrption) are available (mostly used with AS2)

Trading Partner

Same setup what we do in company profile need to be recreate here. Except, we can ignore the communication part (channel details/type system). Just adding the reference of the communication partner is sufficient.

 
 

Here in the identifier for trading partner I have used group of identifiers instead of individual identifier (nothing intresting just because I have tried individual).  These Identifier are used to fetch the correct agreement which will have a better monitoring view per trading partner.

 

How to extract Identifiers

Ok, we have discussed about how to maintain identifer, but how to find the identfier the a little complex. 

  • Company Profile
    • Control Records from Idoc should be the first choice as that have all the Sender/Receiver information such as Logical system, Sender port, Receiver partner profile code etc. 
      • For Company Profile (Outbound) : As generally we have a single company profile so we should choose a value which is common accross all the Outbound flow (Logical system/XML Port are preferable)
      • For Company Profile (Inbound) : Preferable option is the Logical System which we pass in RCVPRN
  • Trading Partner Profile
    • Check the file which you will be consuming and the section where Sender/Receiver information is being transfer 
      StandardSegmentSender Position

      Receiver Position

      EDIFACTUNBS002 (4th element)

      S003 (5th element)

      ANSI X12ISAISA06ISA08
      VDA (Classic)511Pos 4-12Pos 13-21
      EDIWheelBuyer/Seller Party--
      Customized formatSender/Receiver section  

 

Next series of this blog will be on how agreements can be create (will not be too standard as I have faced couple of challange) and additional paramters which are useful. Also, the configuration we made in this part, how these play a valuable role and where these will be visible at runtime. 

link (will be update once published) : Implementing B2B Advisory in SAP Integration Suite for Custom XML EDIFACT Scenarios part - 2

 

Thanks

Aman Sharma

entry.title: Re: Situation Handling for Maintenance Order – Trigger No...
entry.content:
Good day @sriram_00770 

Based on your screenshots, in the standard situation type EAM_MAINT_ORDER_PHASE_CHANGED, the object filters exposed in the app do not include Order Phase and System Status. This is a template limitation in Public Cloud.

See the following references for more details: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/fdbf5eabf0e84621a15c6965b3e01649.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2dfa044a255f49e89a3050daf3c61c11/9e5578397eff4a6588eba5fc1f2ddf56.html

The Public Cloud allows you to notify user when maintenance order changes phase in general by using standard situation type Maintenance Order Phase Changed. You can also filter by the available header level attributes and therefore allowing you to somewhat narrow the scope.

The currently exposed filter fields and the details on your screenshot, you cannot directly trigger only for “Post Execution” phase while using the standard condition builder, you cannot filter by CNF/TECO system status in the standard anchor object filters. Therefore, you cannot achieve a clean phase specific trigger with standard configuration alone in this template. A core limitation.

 

Question 1: How can we trigger a situation notification specifically when a maintenance order enters the post-execution stage in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?

Answer: The standard template can trigger on maintenance order phase change events, but since Order Phase is not exposed as filter, you cannot restrict the trigger to only post execution.

Question 2: Is there any standard workaround or recommended approach to achieve this using Situation Handling?

Answer: Yes, but it is a workaround, not an exact filter-based solution. It would be for you to use EAM_MAINT_ORDER_PHASE_CHANGED and restrict as much as possible with available filters. Then configure recipients, so only production relevant users receive it. A trade off could be that some users may receive notification for multiple phase changes, not only post execution.

Alternatively, you modify your operational process rule by:

  • Triggering on phase changes generally
  • In the notification text, clearly instruct your users to resume production only when the order is in Post Execution/completed maintenance step.
  • Users verify current order phase in the maintenance order app before they carry out any action.

Question 3: Is this a known limitation by design for maintenance orders in Public Cloud?

Answer: Yes. See the following references for more details: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/fdbf5eabf0e84621a15c6965b3e01649.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2dfa044a255f49e89a3050daf3c61c11/9e5578397eff4a6588eba5fc1f2ddf56.html

So yes, this is effectively a standard template limitation by design in Public Cloud. If your business needs exact phase filtering, the best path is an SAP feature request to expose Order Phase/System Status as condition fields in the template.

 

Thank you for your question.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: How to hide "Compensation Structure" object for some countries/legal entities?
entry.content:
Hi Experts

We have a requirement to create a "Compensation Structure" object for a country. The object is created via Manage Data.

This object needs to be displayed on the "Compensation Information" screen in EC and is relevant for only one country. The object has been successfully created and is visible on Compensation Information screen.

As this object is not required for other countries apart from one country, I have tried to write a business rule for hiding this field for "onView" scenario and it works perfectly fine. 

There is also a need to hide the field for other countries for "onInit" scenario and the rule does not seem to work.

This object "Compensation Structure" is a standard out of box object, it also does not allow a custom UI to be created where an "onInit" rule can be applied.

Furthermore, I have had to add this field at the "compinfo" level HRIS element and not at the country-level HRIS element as doing so at the country-level does not allow the "Compensation Structure" object to be reportable. This is product gap duly acknowledged by SAP.

Could you please help suggest how to hide this object for "onInit" scenario where the Compensation Information screen when being rendered first time without any Compensation information values is correctly able to hide this field for all countries minus one.

Any help/suggested is much much appreciated.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Gaming
entry.content:
Love that energy 😄  those “random deep dive” hobbies are the best.

I haven’t fallen into the Warhammer 40,000 or Warhammer Age of Sigmar rabbit hole myself (yet), but I totally get the obsession cycle. One week it’s something casual, and the next you’re learning full lore timelines and faction histories at 2 AM.

Funny enough, my current spiral isn’t miniatures it’s the Letter Boxed unlimited word puzzle from The New York Times. It sounds tame compared to Warhammer, but it turns into the same kind of mental rabbit hole:

  • You start “just solving the daily puzzle.”

  • Then you optimize for two-word solutions.

  • Then you track solve times.

  • Then suddenly you’re analyzing letter distribution patterns like it’s tabletop strategy.

Different battlefield, same tactical brain energy 😅

Honestly, there’s something similar about both hobbies:

  • Warhammer → strategic positioning, long-term planning, deep lore.

  • Letter Boxed → constraint-based thinking, pattern recognition, vocabulary strategy.

Both scratch that “I must master this system” itch.

So no painted armies on my desk yet but definitely a growing spreadsheet of word puzzle stats.

What faction (or grand alliance) pulled you in first?

entry.title: Re: How to reprocess the full language installation ?
entry.content:
In ST05 (or any transaction code you like), start the menu System > Status > Double-click the GUI Status > menu Goto > Translation and select the translations from German to English. You may also use SE63: in S/4HANA 2023, the main ST05 program is R_ST05_TRACE_MAIN, in SE63 type "T" + Enter in the command field then select LIMU CUAD R_ST05_TRACE_MAIN and language from deDE to enUS. In my "French system", I can see that the translations exist in English but not in French. If the French translations exist in your system, then reset the language buffer (try RSLANG20, then SAPRSEUG). If only the English translations exist, check that the profile parameter zcsa/second_language is set to "E" in RZ11 (if yes, reset the language buffer).

EDIT: you have said "SMLT has not been done correctly", do you mean the log shows error messages or you just don't see few texts translated?

entry.title: Configure Your SAP Fiori Elements Apps: Introducing Fine-Tuning in the SAP Fiori Development Portal
entry.content:
Throughout our blog series on the SAP Fiori development portal, we've explored how SAP Fiori elements leverages metadata and annotations to build enterprise-grade UIs following the Global UX design principles. But what happens when you need to adjust UI behavior in ways that aren't expressed through annotations? That's what we call configuration – and the Fiori Tools are the best place for it. But we're excited to introduce a new feature in the SAP Fiori development portal that makes configuration discovery and experimentation easier than ever.

Understanding Fine-Tuning in SAP Fiori Elements

SAP Fiori elements applications are primarily driven by metadata and annotations - this is their superpower. Annotations define what data to display, how to structure it, and which features to enable. However, not every UI behavior can or should be controlled through annotations. Some aspects require configuration settings that fine-tune how building blocks render and behave.

Let's take the table building block as a perfect example. When you annotate your service with UI.LineItem, SAP Fiori elements automatically selects an appropriate table type based on your entity's capabilities and the Global UX design guidelines:

  • If your entity set is analytics-enabled, an Analytical Table is displayed, providing built-in aggregation and grouping capabilities
  • For standard use cases, the Responsive Table is shown by default - it's optimized for various screen sizes and delivers the best user experience across devices
  • But what if you need to display massive datasets where scrolling performance is critical? The Grid Table excels at rendering huge amounts of data efficiently

Here's the challenge: there's no annotation to switch between these table types. This is where fine-tuning comes into play. You configure these settings in your application's manifest.json or directly in the building block definition when using XML views. But how do you discover what's configurable? What are the available options? What values can you set?

Introducing the Fine-Tuning Panel in the SAP Fiori Development Portal

The SAP Fiori development portal now includes a dedicated Fine-Tuning panel that answers these questions interactively. When you open the code editor for any building block - let's use the table as our example - you'll notice two tabs at the top: "Code" and "Fine-Tuning."

Fine-Tuning Panel

The Fine-Tuning panel reveals all available configuration settings for the building block, complete with:

  • Clear descriptions explaining what each setting controls
  • The current value applied in the sample
  • A searchable interface to quickly find specific settings
  • Immediate visual feedback when you change values

Exploring Fine-Tuning: A Table Story

Let's walk through a practical scenario. Navigate to the Table Building Block in the SAP Fiori development portal and click "Show Code" to open the code editor. Now switch to the "Fine-Tuning" tab.

You'll see a comprehensive list of configuration options. Let's say you're working on an application that needs to display thousands of travel records, and scrolling performance is becoming an issue. You remember hearing about the Grid Table's superior performance with large datasets, but you're not sure how to enable it.

In the Fine-Tuning panel, use the search box and type "type." The list immediately filters to show the "type" setting, which controls the table visualization. You'll see:

  • A description explaining the table type setting
  • The current value: "ResponsiveTable" (the default)
  • A dropdown showing all available options: ResponsiveTable, GridTable and AnalyticalTable

Searching for settings

Change the value to "GridTable" and watch the magic happen. The preview immediately updates, showing your table rendered as a Grid Table.

Changing the table type

You can see the visual differences

  • the denser layout
  • the different scrolling behavior
  • the optimized rendering for large datasets

But here's where it gets even better. Switch back to the "Code" tab, and you'll notice the code has been automatically updated:

<core:FragmentDefinition
xmlns:core="sap.ui.core"
xmlns="sap.m"
xmlns:macros="sap.fe.macros">
   <macros:Table metaPath="/Travel/@com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.LineItem" type="GridTable"/>
</core:FragmentDefinition>

The `type="GridTable"` attribute has been added automatically. If you're using building blocks directly in your XML views, you can simply copy this code into your application. Perfect for custom page development!

From Exploration to Implementation

The Fine-Tuning panel serves as your bridge from exploration to implementation. You can experiment with different settings, understand their effects, and see the exact code needed - all in one interactive environment.

However, it's important to understand where these settings belong in your application architecture:

  • For Building Blocks in XML Views (like in custom pages): Apply the settings directly as attributes on the building block, just as shown in the code editor. The portal gives you the exact syntax you need.
  • For Standard Floorplans (List Report, Object Page, etc.): The table building blocks are already integrated into the page structure. To fine-tune them, you add configuration settings to your application's manifest.json file. The SAP Fiori tools Page Map provides a user-friendly interface for configuring these settings without manually editing JSON.

This two-path approach ensures you can fine-tune your application whether you're building custom pages with building blocks or extending standard floorplans.

The Fiori Development Portal as Your Fine-Tuning Laboratory

Think of the SAP Fiori development portal's Fine-Tuning panel as your laboratory for configuration experimentation. You can:

  • Discover settings you didn't know existed
  • Understand the purpose and impact of each configuration option
  • Test different combinations to find the optimal setup for your requirements
  • Learn the exact syntax needed for implementation
  • Make informed decisions backed by visual evidence

This interactive approach transforms configuration from a trial-and-error process into a guided, informed decision-making experience.

But keep in mind: SAP Fiori elements applies Global UX design principles automatically. Only fine-tune when you have specific requirements that justify deviating from the defaults.

What's Next

The Fine-Tuning panel represents another step in our commitment to making SAP Fiori elements development more accessible, efficient, and collaborative. But we're not stopping here. We have exciting enhancements planned that will further improve your development experience with the SAP Fiori development portal.

Stay tuned for upcoming blog posts where we'll introduce additional features that continue to bridge the gap between exploration and implementation, making your journey from concept to production even smoother.

To get the full overview of the SAP Fiori development portal, check out the following blog posts of the mini-series:

  • Introducing the SAP Fiori Development Portal: Your Gateway to Rapid SAP Fiori App Creation (1 of 6)
  • Get familiar with SAP Fiori development portal and the power of the building blocks (2 of 6)
  • Explore the potential of SAP Fiori development portal using complex building blocks (3 of 6)
  • Use SAP Fiori development portal to understand extension options for standard floorplans (4 of 6)
  • Prepare building custom pages by using SAP Fiori development portal (5 of 6)
  • Bringing the SAP Fiori development portal and its use to the next level (6 of 6)

entry.title: Re: Payment Advice API - Field Mapping Questions for Paym...
entry.content:
Good day @byd 

Thank you for your questions.

Below you will find answers to the specific points.

Question 1: In the Manage Payment Advice app (F2550), what does the "Payment Reference" field in the header represent? Is it the Accounting Document ID or the Billing Document ID? Please clarify explicitly.

Answer: It is not reliably the Accounting Document ID or Billing Document ID by definition. In the advice context, Payment Reference is generally a reference value used for payment identification, not guaranteed document number field for FI accounting document or SD billing document. So, the safest explicit answer is Header “Payment Reference” equals payment reference value, not inherently “Accounting Document ID” and not inherently “Billing Document ID”

Question 2: In the Manage Payment Advice app (F2550), what does the "Reference" field in the line items represent? Is it the Accounting Document ID or the Billing Document ID? Please clarify explicitly.

Answer: By definition, this is not universally equal to Accounting Document ID or Billing Document ID. The line-item reference in payment advice is typically a reference field carried for the payment ad ice item, but don’t assume it always mean Account Document ID or Billing Document ID without checking your tenant behaviour and the source process.

Question 3: By populating these fields in the app (F2550), can we establish linkages to related documents that are visible in the document flow?

Answer: Generally, no, not by itself. Populating payment advice reference fields usually stores reference information for payment advice to process and match, but it does not automatically create SD/FI document flow relationships in the same way that standard document references created by integrated business transactions do. So, the basically can support identification but not create linkages on their own.

Question 4: When posting the header "Payment Reference" field via the API (https://api.sap.com/api/API_PAYMENT_ADVICE_SRV/cloud-sdk/ABAP), which specific field in the API payload should be specified?

Answer: The header entity field, which is typically named PaymentReference.

Question 5: When posting the line item "Reference" field via the API (https://api.sap.com/api/API_PAYMENT_ADVICE_SRV/cloud-sdk/ABAP), which specific field in the API payload should be specified?

Answer: The item entity field, which is Reference.

 

I suggest you validate in your test tenant before going to production

See the following resources for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/4f22209d1f5d4bd79c4a55017608b51b/f8f29bb9d08a418d936ce5de8a78b33f.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/4f22209d1f5d4bd79c4a55017608b51b/f7fa16ce4b7e4059a1ac7cab9dd907af.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/4f22209d1f5d4bd79c4a55017608b51b/5b8b271ca8004116bef1fe2c462076b0.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/cdccca8e03d74101a0135863bc522b49/a80bc5536a51204be10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: How to inherit value of field (BSEG-PAYT_RSN) while c...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. I'm very sorry for the delay.

In this case, I asked an internal chatbot for support. Here's the response:

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, when creating a residual item in the payment clearing app (F1367),  the ‘Payment Reason’ field (BSEG-PAYT_RSN) from the original accounts payable (AP) cleared item is not automatically inherited to the newly created AP residual items. This behavior is due to the standard system design, and currently, there is no direct configuration or substitution method available in the cloud environment to change this behavior. For more information, see 2784407 - How to make residual clearing in ‘Post Incoming Payments’ | SAP Knowledge Base Article.

To address this issue, you may consider the following options:

  1. Manual Entry: After creating the residual item, manually enter the ‘Payment Reason’ in the new item if the system allows editing.

  2. Custom Development: If manual entry is not feasible, you might need to explore custom development options. 

  3. Feedback to SAP: If this functionality is critical, consider providing feedback to SAP through the Customer Influence Portal. 

I hope this helps.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Early-Career SAP Path: PMO vs Functional Consultant
entry.content:
I’m seeking guidance on the best early-career path in SAP. A brief overview of my background:

  • Education: Bachelor’s in Business Information Technology

  • Experience: 1-year internship at DHL (SHAPE+ project), supporting a global SAP S/4HANA Finance transformation in a PMO capacity managing backlog, change requests, workshops, and stakeholder communication. I also have a 1 year prior experience in ERP Implementation Project (Salesforce).

  • Skills & Certifications: CAPM, Google Project Management, Scrum Fundamentals, Six Sigma Yellow Belt, SAP Activate Project Management (in progress), Jira, Confluence, MS Project, Power BI.

I’m debating whether to target SAP PMO roles now or pivot into hands-on functional consulting first. My concern is that switching to functional consulting without prior experience in SAP modules (FI/CO/SD/MM/EWM, etc.) could feel like starting from scratch, even though I already have exposure to SAP projects in a PMO context.

Specifically, I’d love insights on:

  1. Are there genuine early-career opportunities in SAP PMO?

  2. How competitive are these roles for someone with PMO internship experience but limited functional expertise?

  3. From your experience, is it better to gain hands-on functional experience first, or can a PMO-focused career be viable at this stage?

Any advice, personal experiences, or recommendations would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance!
Salim Swaleh

entry.title: Re: Standard Exchange Rates (CDS) fails when executing th...
entry.content:
Hey @Minthang1 ,

The error you are getting is related to the source data - at least according to the error message you included. 

When a field is marked as a key in Datasphere, it automatically becomes non-nullable , which means that it will fail if you try to add a record with an empty GDATU, it will fail.

To the best of my knowledge, TCURR also enforces non-null for GDATU, so it's highly unlikely that your source data contains a null value, but the standard data flow dosen't contain any logic that would create null values.

Just to be sure, I would suggest to go to the source system, open TCURR, and verify that the tables does not contain either null OR 00000000, which Datasphere would interpret af an empty date.

entry.title: Re: SmartForm Recovery – Request for Assistance
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. I'm very sorry for the delay.

In this case, I asked an internal chatbot for support. Here's the response:

Restoring Previous Version of SmartForm

To restore a previous version of a SmartForm in SAP, you can follow these steps:

  1. Version Management: SAP provides a version management feature for SmartForms. You can check if there are any previous versions saved. To do this, go to the SmartForm transaction (e.g., SMARTFORMS), enter the name of your SmartForm, and look for the version management option. This will allow you to view and potentially restore previous versions if they exist.

  2. Transport Requests: If you have previously transported the SmartForm changes using a transport request, you can attempt to re-import the old transport request. However, as you mentioned, this did not reflect the previous changes. Ensure that the transport request contains the correct version of the SmartForm you wish to restore.

  3. Backup Systems: If your organization maintains backup systems or snapshots, you might be able to retrieve the previous version of the SmartForm from there.

  4. Manual Recreation: If none of the above methods work, you may need to manually recreate the previous version of the SmartForm based on documentation or screenshots, if available.

Unfortunately, if the version management does not have the previous version or if the transport request does not contain the desired changes, it may not be possible to automatically restore the previous version without manual intervention.

I hope this helps.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: "Getting Started: Maximizing Your Potential from the Start"
entry.content:
Overview of the three main content areas and how they support your development.

  1. Discussions – Learn Together
    Connect with peers to ask questions, share knowledge, and collaborate on learning. Our experts are available to provide guidance and answers if needed.
  2. Blog – Stay Informed and Inspired
    Stay informed on product developments, features, and certification paths with regular updates to enhance your skills.
  3. Knowledge Base – Official Learning Updates
    Central source for official announcements related to learning content, including new or updated Learning Journeys, certification changes, new course releases, and invitations to live sessions or expert-led trainings.

Tour the SAP Community - Explore the various features, functionality, and aspects of the SAP Community, familiarize yourself with community guidelines, and discover how to get the most out of your membership.

START Learning based on your proficiency level

  1. Start with the Basics
    Positioning SAP Business Suite     - Begin with a general overview to understand the positioning and value of SAP Business Suite. This will give you a solid foundation before moving into specific topics.
  2. Choose Your Line of Business
    Next, focus on the area that interests you most    . This helps you understand the solutions and processes within your chosen domain. For example:
    • Positioning SAP Financial Management Solutions
    • Positioning SAP Supply Chain Management Solutions
  3. Deepen Your Knowledge
    Continue with a specific Line of Business and deepen your expertise. For example:
    • Positioning Enterprise Performance Management
    • Positioning Financial Planning & Analysis
    • Positioning Accounting & Financial Close
    • Positioning the SAP Business Suite for Governance, Risk and Compliance
  4. Become an Expert

    • SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
    • SAP Certified Specialist - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

Recommended Communities

We invite you to become a member and subscribe to the communities below for in-depth discussions on Finance and Supply Chain Management::

  • Welcome to our Financial Management Learning Group - SAP Community
  • Welcome to our Supply Chain Management Learning Gr... - SAP Community

Happy Learning 😊

entry.title: Re: How to create an XML structure of header and items in...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. I suppose this is the information you're looking for?

RAP Data Services for Print Forms

In addition, I asked our internal chatbot for support. Here's the response:

To create an XML structure of header and items in RAP Core for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you need to ensure that both the header and item entities are correctly defined and linked in your CDS views and behavior definitions. Here is a step-by-step guide:

Step 1: Define CDS Views 

  1. Header Entity: Define a CDS view for the header entity. This should include all necessary fields for the header.

    define root view entity ZRAP_Header
as select from your_header_table
{
  key id as HeaderID,
  field1,
  field2,
  ...
}

  2. Item Entity: Define a CDS view for the item entity. This should include a foreign key to the header entity.

    define view entity ZRAP_Item
as select from your_item_table
{
  key id as ItemID,
  header_id as HeaderID,  // Foreign key to header
  item_field1,
  item_field2,
  ...
}

Step 2: Define Behavior

  1. Header Behavior: Define the behavior for the header entity, including the association to the item entity.

    define behavior for ZRAP_Header alias Header
{
  use create;
  use update;
  use delete;
  association [0..*] to ZRAP_Item as _Items on _Items.HeaderID = HeaderID;
}

  2. Item Behavior: Define the behavior for the item entity.

    define behavior for ZRAP_Item alias Item
{
  use create;
  use update;
  use delete;
}

Step 3: Service Definition and Binding

  1. Service Definition: Expose the header and item entities in a service definition.

    define service ZRAP_Service
{
  expose ZRAP_Header as Headers;
  expose ZRAP_Item as Items;
}

  2. Service Binding: Create a service binding to expose the service as an OData service.

Step 4: Test the Application

  • Use the Fiori Elements app to test the application. Ensure that the UI annotations are correctly set to display both header and item data.

By following these steps, you should be able to create a form that displays both header and item data in your RAP application.

Additional information: ABAP Restful Application Programming Model (RAP) - SAP Community

I hope this helps.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Calibration Dashboard View – Configuration Options
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
 
I am currently working with Calibration Templates in SSFF and I need clarification regarding the Dashboard View configuration. My issue is specifically related to the Dashboard View, not the Matrix Grid View.
 
When I access the Dashboard section within the template configuration, the area appears visible but completely non-editable. I am not able to modify the order of the charts, edit what is displayed, or adjust any configuration settings. The section appears essentially read-only.
However, in several SAP Help pages and learning materials, I have seen examples of the Calibration Dashboard displayed with different visual styles and color schemes. In our instance, the dashboard uses strong, intense colors, whereas in SAP documentation examples, the dashboards appear in lighter or softer tones.
 
This brings us my main concerns: the dashboard displays (including the order of the evaluated attributes/ratings shown in the charts) are not fully consistent with what we would expect; and I cannot find any configuration options to adjust layout, order, or visual appearance.
 
Based on what I have been reading in the documentation, I would like to confirm:
1- Is the Calibration Dashboard View fully system-generated and non-configurable?
2- Is there any way to configure the charts, their order, or visual properties such as colors and layout?
3- Is this controlled via XML in the template, theme/branding settings, or any separate configuration tool?
 
These are mainly related to user experience and internal compliance requirements.
 
I am including some reference links below as examples of the differences in color and visual presentation that I have observed in SAP documentation and learning materials: 
Configuración de vistas de plantilla de calibración
Performing Calibration with SAP SuccessFactors Performance Manage
 
Any clarification or official documentation reference would be greatly appreciated.
 
Thank you in advance!
 
SAP SuccessFactors Performance & Goals

entry.title: Re: Accruals on subscription licenses
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. I'm very sorry for the delay.

I asked an internal chatbot for support. The following information sources were used:

Accrual and Deferral Posting with Purchase Order in S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition

Accruals Management - Purchase Order Accruals and Deferrals

Here's the response:

To address your requirement of deferring and recognizing purchase costs over the subscription term using the same recognition logic as revenue, you can consider the following approach:

  1. Purchase Order Creation:

    • Create purchase orders in S/4HANA for the software licenses you resell. This will allow you to perform a 3-way match with the supplier invoices and link the costs to the revenue for margin reporting.

  2. Accrual and Deferral Management:

    • Utilize the Accruals Management functionality in S/4HANA  to automate the calculation and posting of accruals and deferrals. This can be configured to match the revenue recognition method you are using, ensuring consistent margins across periods.
    • The system can calculate accruals and deferrals based on planned costs in the purchase order, allowing for a linear distribution of costs over the subscription period. This is similar to the revenue recognition process you described.

  3. Matching Revenue and Cost Recognition:

    • Ensure that the deferral method for costs matches the revenue recognition method by configuring the accrual engine to use the same logic. This can be done by setting up the accrual item types and methods to align with your revenue recognition setup.

  4. Integration with Subscription Billing:

    • If you are using SAP Subscription Billing, ensure that the integration with S/4HANA is set up to facilitate the seamless flow of data between the systems. This will help in aligning the cost and revenue recognition processes.

By following these steps, you can achieve a consistent and accurate reflection of margins across periods, aligning the cost recognition with the revenue recognition logic you are using.

I hope this helps.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Naming/Set up company code ID
entry.content:
Dear @marcel_f,

Point 1. There was no hard switch that makes numeric company codes invalid

In Public Cloud the company code key is still 4-character alphanumeric 4-digit alphanumeric ID in the help text. That means purely numeric IDs like 1010 are still valid

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b249d650b15e4b3d9fc2077ee921abd0/22fc75c2e32b429f8791940933d3961e.html

Point 2. What did change is mainly the recommended / guided naming convention in CBC

The latest CBC guide explains that SAP uses country/region codes and IDs to create country/region-specific values and that a value can consist of:

  • the country/region code or ID alone or
  • the code/ID plus additional characters

For example, the guide’s country/region table shows Germany = code DE, country/region ID 10, so 10DE is simply 10 (country/region ID) + DE (country code), which matches the convention

Also, in the Set Up Organizational Structure procedure, the guide explicitly instructs for the company code:

  • ID: <Country/Region ID> and in practice you then complete it to 4 chars with additional characters as needed

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/doc/c38e987da8cf471f8888d703938f2ef1/SHIP3.0/en-US/SAPCentralBusinessConfiguration_ProjectExperience_en-US.pdf

Point 3. Why you saw Starter vs Customer differences

This is typically not because numeric is forbidden but because:

  • Starter/evaluation systems often have pre-delivered demo IDs so 1010 may already exist pushing you to pick another ID like 10DE and/or
  • guided CBC flows steer you toward the country/region ID-based pattern

Summary:

  • Both 1010 and 10DE can still be possible in Public Cloud as long as the ID is 4 characters and not already used
  • SAP current guidance is to align new org-unit IDs to country/region ID + optional extra characters which is why you increasingly see 10DE-style IDs
  • The note you attached is not strictly for the Public Cloud version

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Cash Flow Report in SAP Business One
entry.content:
The Cash Flow report is one of the key reports you can find among the financial reports in SAP Business One. However, there is not much information on how to use this report for your key business solutions.

First at all – why is it so important?

The Cash Flow report lets you see and foresee your liquidity balance for a certain period of time with different security levels1 from cash accounts to Business Partners forecast. The following diagram represents the security level of different components that affect your liquidity from the point of view of SAP Business One: 

Let’s see how this information is displayed in the report in SAP Business One2.: 

Like all other reports, the Cash Flow report requires you to enter the time frame to collect data. So, this is the first mandatory step. In this example, I run the report in the middle of the period, so I can see the real data for February and future data for March.

With weekly intervals, I can see the following results:

There are 9 collapsed lines for every 7 days starting from February 1. In the report header you can select or deselect different security levels. The effect of that can be seen in the results. Here you can see the difference once I deselect customer liabilities: 

Customer liabilities are taken away as a liquidity source and as a result there is cash shortage by the end of February. Let’s take a closer look at the customer liabilities now by expanding the relevant week and security level to understand why it is at the end of February and not earlier or later.  

The Invoice 1064 was issued in January with total amount 2,120.00 $. However, in the report it is displayed only in February and only a half of the total amount. This is because amount 1,060.00 $ is taken from the installments table of this Invoice: 

Let’s check how the data changes in case you have some statistics about business partner payment experience and you want to consider that in your report. So, this business partner has this information already in his master data: 

 And going back to the selection criteria you can add one more criterion: 

 And see results: the customer liabilities are now moved one week later –  You can also run the report only for specific business partners in case it is required. For that you can update your selection criteria on the Business Partner tab: In this way, SAP Business One will check only the documents that are issued to the business partners you have selected.

Let’s check another security level: cash accounts. First of all, we can select or deselect specific accounts on the selection criteria Cash tab:

entry.title: Re: SAP DM Production Process Design Read Indicator Dummy...
entry.content:
Hi @aziz_khan_,

I’m assuming that you have successfully completed the integration between SAP DM, SAP CC, and the SAP Production Connector.

My suggestion is: if you are familiar with Node.js or with programming in general, I recommend running a Node.js OPC UA server using the following project:
https://github.com/node-opcua/node-opcua
It’s very easy to set up and does not require advanced programming skills. You simply need to install it and run the server— it already includes a dummy subset of tags that are ready for read/write operations.

With this setup, you can connect your own server to SAP DM and experiment with tag read/write using SAP DM PPD.

Marco

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to query dimension and measure metadat...
entry.content:
Hello @christoffer-ebbesen ,

I think we need a bit more information to be able to help you. 

Is there a way to query metadata on measures and dimensions in SAP datasphere? Specifically the business/technical names of the measures and dimensions, and what view they belong to. 

For simple Business / Technical names, then yes - you can use the /$metadata endpoint to find all that.
Note: It returns an XML-file, and not a JSON file. This is mainly because SAP wants to see you suffer. Anyway.

In the metadata, you're find the technical name and the data type:

 

If you are asking for the lineage of a field, then sadly no. 
I gave a more detailed answer here. 

 

Right now i'm extracting this data into snowflake through the OData relational API, just to reimport it back into datasphere. It seems stupid to extract data from datasphere just to import back into the same system. 

Yes, that seems like a god-awful waste of time. Quick question though - Why? 
Are you doing transformations in snowflake, renaming columns?
